name: Return

aliases.name: ReturnF
aliases.type: Return msg model -> Return msg model

values.name: andMap
values.comment: Map an `Return` into a `Return` containing a `Model` function
values.type: Return msg (a -> b) -> Return msg a -> Return msg b

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain together expressions from `Model` to `Return`.

```elm
-- arbitrary function to demonstrate
foo : Model -> Return Msg Model
foo ({bar} as model) =
  -- forking logic
  if bar < 10
  -- that side effects may be added
  then (model, getAjaxThing)
  -- that the model may be updated
  else ({model | bar = model.bar - 2 }, Cmd.none)
```

They are now chainable with `andThen`...

```elm
resulting : Return msg { model | bar : Int }
resulting =
  myReturn `andThen` foo
           `andThen` foo
           `andThen` foo
```

Here we changed up `foo` three times, but we can use any function of
type `(a -> Return msg b)`.

Commands will be accumulated automatically as is the case with all
functions in this library.
values.type: Return msg a -> (a -> Return msg b) -> Return msg b

values.name: ask
values.type: Return msg model -> Return msg (Cmd msg)

values.name: command
values.comment: Add a `Cmd` to a `Return`, the `Model` is uneffected
values.type: Cmd msg -> Return msg model -> Return msg model

values.name: effect
values.comment: Add a `Cmd` to a `Return` based on its `Model`, the `Model` will not be effected
values.type: (model -> Cmd msg) -> Return msg model -> Return msg model

values.name: flatten
values.type: Return msg (Return msg model) -> Return msg model

values.name: listen
values.type: Return msg a -> Return msg (Return msg a)

values.name: map
values.comment: Transform the `Model` the `Cmd` will be left untouched
values.type: (a -> b) -> Return msg a -> Return msg b

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Combine 2 `Return`s with a function

```elm
map2
  (\modelA modelB -> { modelA | foo = modelB.foo })
  retA
  retB
```
values.type: (a -> b -> c) -> Return msg a -> Return msg b -> Return msg c

values.name: map3
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d) -> Return msg a -> Return msg b -> Return msg c -> Return msg d

values.name: map4
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e) -> Return msg a -> Return msg b -> Return msg c -> Return msg d -> Return msg e

values.name: map5
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f) -> Return msg a -> Return msg b -> Return msg c -> Return msg d -> Return msg e -> Return msg f

values.name: mapCmd
values.type: (Cmd a -> Cmd b) -> Return a model -> Return b model

values.name: mapWith
values.comment: Transform the `Model` of and add a new `Cmd` to the queue
values.type: (a -> b) -> Cmd msg -> Return msg a -> Return msg b

values.name: pass
values.type: Return msg ( model, Cmd msg -> Cmd msg_ ) -> Return msg_ model

values.name: return
values.type: model -> Cmd msg -> Return msg model

values.name: sequence
values.type: List (Return msg model) -> Return msg (List model)

binops.comment:
Go point free with `andThen` chaining. Looking at the example from `andThen`

```elm
resulting : Return msg { model | bar : Int }
resulting =
  myReturn `andThen` foo
           `andThen` foo
           `andThen` foo
```

this code roughly becomes:

```elm
doFoo3Times : { model | bar : Int } -> Return msg { model | bar : Int }
doFoo3Times =
  foo >>> foo >>> foo
```
binops.type: (b -> Return msg c) -> (a -> Return msg b) -> a -> Return msg c
(a -> Return msg b) -> (b -> Return msg c) -> a -> Return msg c
Return msg a -> (a -> Return msg b) -> Return msg b

binops.comment: Same as `>>|` but the left hand `Model` is dropped
binops.type: Return msg model -> Return msg model_ -> Return msg model_

binops.comment: Same as `>>|` but the right hand `Model` is dropped
binops.type: Return msg model_ -> Return msg model -> Return msg model_
(a -> Return msg b) -> Return msg a -> Return msg b
